key_drivers: VIX at 18.41 — below historical crash-preceding levels (>25)
Record margin debt ($1.42T) creates forced-selling vulnerability
S&P 500 RSI neutral at 48 — no overbought trigger
5_day.interpretation: Near-term crash risk is low. VIX is subdued and no imminent catalysts are visible. However, record leverage means any sudden shock could cascade faster than usual.

key_drivers: CAPE at 41x and Buffett Indicator at 234% — extreme valuations amplify downside
Negative equity risk premium (-1.9%) — stocks not compensating for bond alternative
Quarter-end rebalancing pressure evident in recent tech selloff
Private credit defaults at record 6% — potential contagion channel
20_day.interpretation: Moderately elevated risk over 20 trading days. Extreme valuations provide no cushion against negative surprises, and institutional quarter-end positioning adds tactical risk.

key_drivers: Margin debt YoY growth of 54% at record levels — historical precursor to forced deleveraging
Buffett Indicator near all-time high (234% vs median 81%)
Private credit stress (6% default rate) could trigger broader risk repricing
Yield curve flattening (0.31%) suggests late-cycle dynamics
Semiconductor sector showing vulnerability (KLAC short interest +1060%)
60_day.interpretation: Structural risks are significant over 60 trading days. The combination of record leverage, extreme valuations, and private credit stress creates conditions where a catalyst could trigger a cascading sell-off. However, absence of yield curve inversion and moderate VIX suggest the timing remains uncertain.

crash_probability.methodology: Based on current VIX level, technical indicators, macro conditions, historical base rates, and market structure analysis.

metadata.data_source: Yahoo Finance (yfinance), CBOE, FRED, CNN, FINRA, Fitch Ratings, GuruFocus
metadata.disclaimer: This analysis is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

vix.name: CBOE Volatility Index (VIX)
vix.interpretation: VIX at 18.41 is slightly below the historical average of ~20. Implies moderate market anxiety but no panic. Recent 52-week range shows capacity for spikes to 31+.

put_call_ratio.name: CBOE Equity Put/Call Ratio
put_call_ratio.interpretation: Equity put/call ratio at 0.85 indicates moderately elevated hedging activity. Above the long-term average of 0.7, suggesting cautious positioning — a potential contrarian bullish signal.

fear_greed_index.name: CNN Fear & Greed Index
fear_greed_index.interpretation: Fear & Greed at 28 is in 'Fear' territory. Markets are pricing in significant pessimism, which historically has been a contrarian bullish indicator. Dropped sharply from 71 in early May.

dalio_bubble_indicator.name: Dalio-Style Bubble Indicator
dalio_bubble_indicator.interpretation: Composite score of 65.3 indicates 'Bubble' conditions. Valuations (Gauge 1: 94.0) and unsustainable conditions (Gauge 2: 95) are extreme, while sentiment gauges (3-4) are depressed due to current Fear regime. Leverage (Gauge 5: 93.0) is highly elevated at record margin debt levels.

prices_vs_traditional_measures.description: How high are prices relative to traditional measures (CAPE, Buffett Indicator)?

unsustainable_conditions.description: Are prices discounting unsustainable conditions (equity risk premium)?

new_buyer_entry.description: How many new buyers have entered the market (retail participation proxy)?

bullish_sentiment.description: How broadly bullish is sentiment (Fear & Greed, RSI)?

leverage_purchases.description: Are purchases being financed by high leverage (margin debt)?

forward_purchases.description: Have buyers made extended forward purchases (capex, forward speculation)?

dalio_bubble_indicator.methodology: Rules-based proxy of Ray Dalio's 6-gauge bubble framework. Each gauge scores 0-100 using CAPE, Buffett Indicator, equity risk premium, Fear & Greed Index, RSI, and margin debt metrics. Composite is equal-weighted average.
dalio_bubble_indicator.source_note: Rules-based proxy — not Bridgewater's proprietary indicator. Inspired by Dalio's publicly described 6-gauge framework.

shiller_cape.name: Shiller CAPE Ratio (Cyclically Adjusted P/E)
shiller_cape.interpretation: CAPE at 41.02 is 141% above the long-term historical mean of 17x and approaching the all-time high of 44.2x (Dec 2021). Only the 1999-2000 dot-com era and 2021 saw comparable valuations. Implies muted long-term real returns.

buffett_indicator.name: Buffett Indicator (Total Market Cap / GDP)
buffett_indicator.interpretation: At 233.8%, the Buffett Indicator is near its all-time high of 237.8% and far above fair value range (75-115%). Market capitalization vastly exceeds the underlying economy, indicating extreme overvaluation by this metric.

yield_curve_10y2y.name: 10-Year minus 2-Year Treasury Spread
yield_curve_10y2y.interpretation: The yield curve is positive but very flat at 0.31%, well below the long-term average of 0.85%. After a historic inversion that ended in late 2024, the curve has re-steepened but remains compressed, consistent with late-cycle dynamics.
yield_curve_10y2y.recession_signal: The yield curve uninverted ~21 months ago. Historically, recessions follow uninversion by 6-24 months, placing the current period in the late portion of this window. The flat curve (0.31%) signals elevated vigilance.

sp500_technicals.name: S&P 500 Technical Analysis
sp500_technicals.overall_interpretation: S&P 500 is in a neutral-to-cautious technical posture. Price is essentially at the 50DMA (-0.13%) with golden cross intact. RSI at 48 is neutral. Drawdown from ATH is modest at -3.4%, suggesting no panic but the uptrend has stalled.

price_vs_50dma.interpretation: Price essentially at the 50-day moving average — trend momentum is neutral.

price_vs_200dma.interpretation: 6.2% above the 200DMA — long-term uptrend intact but not overextended.

golden_cross.interpretation: Golden cross active (50DMA at 7,363 > 200DMA at 6,925). Bullish long-term trend confirmation.

rsi_assessment.interpretation: RSI at 48 is dead-center neutral. Neither overbought nor oversold, suggesting a consolidation phase.

drawdown_assessment.interpretation: 3.4% drawdown from the all-time high of 7,609.78 — a normal pullback, not yet a correction (>10%).

sp500_above_50dma.name: S&P 500 Stocks Above 50-Day Moving Average
sp500_above_50dma.interpretation: 55% of S&P 500 stocks are above their 50-day MA — near the lower end of normal range. Breadth has narrowed from 82% earlier this year, indicating growing selectivity in the rally.

sp500_above_200dma.name: S&P 500 Stocks Above 200-Day Moving Average (S5TH)
sp500_above_200dma.interpretation: 65% of S&P 500 stocks above their 200-day MA — moderate breadth. Suggests the long-term uptrend is intact for most stocks but leadership is narrowing.

advance_decline_ratio.name: NYSE Advance/Decline Ratio
advance_decline_ratio.interpretation: Advance/Decline ratio of 1.12 (2,529 advancing vs 2,262 declining) indicates slightly more stocks advancing than declining — breadth is positive but not strong.

overall_breadth_assessment.interpretation: Market breadth is adequate but narrowing. The majority of stocks remain in long-term uptrends (65% above 200DMA) but short-term participation has weakened (55% above 50DMA). This narrowing is consistent with late-cycle behavior where fewer stocks drive index-level gains.

finra_margin_debt.name: FINRA Margin Debt
finra_margin_debt.interpretation: Margin debt hit a new all-time high of $1,415.6B in May 2026, with YoY growth of 53.7%. This pace of leverage accumulation is historically extreme and has preceded every major market top. Net investor credit reached a record negative $991.7B.
risks: Record leverage creates forced-selling cascade risk on any significant downturn
YoY growth of 54% far exceeds any sustainable pace — historically peaks precede crashes by 3-12 months
Net negative investor credit of -$992B means aggregate investors owe more than they hold in cash
finra_margin_debt.regulatory_note: FINRA margin requirements remain at 25% maintenance minimum. No regulatory tightening has been implemented despite record leverage levels.

overview.interpretation: Private credit has grown to a $2 trillion market with record-high default rates (6%), creating systemic risk through interconnectedness with banks, insurers, and pension funds. The sector's opacity and limited regulatory oversight amplify potential contagion channels.

default_rates.name: Default Rate
default_rates.interpretation: Fitch's Private Credit Default Rate remained at a record 6% in May 2026, with 14 default events across healthcare, business services, and industrial manufacturing. Elevated above historical averages and showing no signs of improvement.

borrower_health.name: Borrower Health
borrower_health.interpretation: An estimated 42% of private credit borrowers have negative free cash flow, up from 32% in 2021. Deteriorating cash generation capacity signals growing debt service strain across the portfolio.

pik_usage.name: PIK Interest Usage
pik_usage.interpretation: Payment-in-kind interest usage continues to rise as borrowers unable to service cash interest capitalize it into principal. This defers but amplifies default risk, creating a growing cohort of zombie borrowers.
pik_usage.trend: Increasing — estimated 18-22% of private credit loans now using some form of PIK
mechanisms: Full PIK toggle — all interest capitalized into principal
Partial PIK — portion of coupon paid in kind during stress periods
PIK step-ups — higher rate charged but deferred, compounding borrower leverage

liquidity_risk.name: Liquidity Risk
liquidity_risk.interpretation: Several private credit funds have imposed redemption gates or limits. The fundamental liquidity mismatch — illiquid assets in semi-liquid fund structures — creates potential for a run dynamic during market stress.
gated_funds: Multiple BDCs imposed NAV-based redemption limits in 2026
Several interval funds extended redemption queues to 6+ months
At least 3 semi-liquid vehicles suspended new redemptions temporarily
risks: Liquidity mismatch between quarterly/semi-annual redemption windows and multi-year loan durations
Potential forced selling of performing loans to meet redemptions — fire-sale contagion
Retail investors in semi-liquid vehicles may not understand true lock-up risk

bank_interconnectedness.name: Bank Exposure to Private Credit
bank_interconnectedness.interpretation: Major banks have significant direct and indirect exposure to private credit through warehouse lending, fund financing, and co-investment. Total non-bank financial intermediation (NDFI) exposure exceeds $2.5 trillion, creating a transmission channel for private credit stress to reach the regulated banking system.
bank_interconnectedness.growth_since_2010: 180% increase in bank lending to NBFIs since 2010
bank_interconnectedness.bank_commitments_to_pc_funds: $400B+ in committed credit lines to private credit funds
bank_interconnectedness.regulatory_response: Fed and OCC have increased supervisory focus on NBFI interconnectedness; Basel III endgame may increase capital requirements for bank exposures to private funds.

shadow_defaults.name: Shadow Defaults
shadow_defaults.description: Distressed exchanges, liability management exercises (LMEs), and covenant amendments that avoid formal default classification but represent economic impairment.
shadow_defaults.interpretation: Including shadow defaults (LMEs, distressed exchanges, PIK conversions), the true economic impairment rate is estimated at 10-11% — nearly double the official 6% default rate. These mechanisms mask the true extent of borrower distress.
mechanisms: Amend-and-extend — maturity pushed out with modified terms, avoiding default trigger
Distressed debt exchanges — existing lenders swap debt for new instruments at worse terms
Uptiering transactions — new money lenders given priority claim, subordinating existing holders

sectors.name: Healthcare Services
sectors.risk: High — reimbursement cuts, labor costs, regulatory uncertainty

sectors.name: Software/Technology
sectors.risk: Elevated — high multiples, negative FCF, customer concentration

sectors.name: Business Services
sectors.risk: Elevated — cyclical demand sensitivity, thin margins

sectors.name: Industrials/Manufacturing
sectors.risk: Moderate-High — supply chain, input cost inflation

sectors.name: Retail/Consumer
sectors.risk: High — discretionary spending weakness, e-commerce disruption

systemic_risk_assessment.interpretation: Private credit poses elevated systemic risk due to its size ($2T), opacity, record defaults (6%), growing bank interconnectedness, and limited regulatory oversight. While not yet at crisis levels, the structural features that enabled the 2008 crisis — leverage, complexity, illiquidity, and interconnectedness — are present in nascent form.
systemic_risk_assessment.comparison_to_2008: Key differences: lower overall leverage ratios, less securitization layering, better bank capitalization. Key similarities: opacity, liquidity mismatch, regulatory gaps, rapid growth preceding stress, and interconnection with systemically important institutions.
crash_transmission_channels: Fund redemption pressure → forced asset sales → NAV declines → further redemptions (reflexive spiral)
Bank warehouse exposure losses → credit tightening → broader lending pullback
Insurance/pension mark-to-market losses → portfolio reallocation away from risk assets
Confidence contagion — headline defaults trigger risk repricing across all credit
mitigating_factors: No comparable securitization/CDO chain amplifying losses
Fed has established precedent for emergency lending facilities
Most private credit is senior secured — recovery rates should exceed unsecured
regulatory_gaps: No mark-to-market requirement for most private credit — allows extend-and-pretend
Limited disclosure requirements vs public markets
No stress testing framework comparable to bank CCAR
Retail access via semi-liquid vehicles without full risk comprehension

near_term_stress.description: VIX at 18.41, RSI neutral at 48, Fear & Greed in 'Fear' territory (contrarian bullish). No imminent catalyst visible.

valuation_vulnerability.description: CAPE at 41x (93rd percentile historically), Buffett Indicator at 234% near ATH, negative equity risk premium. Extreme overvaluation creates large downside if sentiment shifts.

technical_regime.description: Golden cross intact, price near 50DMA, 6% above 200DMA. Trend structure is neutral — neither clearly bullish nor breaking down. Breadth narrowing is a concern.

market_structure.description: Record margin debt ($1.42T, +54% YoY), private credit defaults at record 6%, KLAC short interest surge. Leverage and credit stress create vulnerability to cascading forced selling.

macro_environment.description: Yield curve positive (0.31%), no recession signal yet. Economy still growing. However, curve is flat and late in the post-inversion window historically associated with recessions.

key_warnings: Margin debt at all-time high with 54% YoY growth — precedes every major top
Negative equity risk premium — stocks offer no compensation over bonds
Private credit defaults at record 6% — shadow banking stress building
Buffett Indicator near all-time high (234%) — extreme overvaluation
Market breadth narrowing — fewer stocks participating in rally
mitigating_factors: VIX subdued at 18 — no panic or extreme fear in options market
Fear & Greed at 28 ('Fear') — contrarian bullish signal
Golden cross intact — long-term trend structure still positive
No yield curve inversion — recession signal not active
Corporate earnings growth supporting elevated multiples short-term

base_case.outlook: Continued consolidation with occasional volatility. S&P 500 trades in a range around current levels (±5%) as markets digest elevated valuations without a major catalyst for crash or breakout.

correction_case.outlook: 10-15% correction over 2-4 months driven by valuation compression. Likely triggered by a catalyst that reprices risk but doesn't create systemic stress.
triggers: Unexpected inflation spike forcing Fed hawkishness

crash_case.outlook: 20%+ decline within 60 days, potentially cascading into bear market. Would require a systemic trigger that forces rapid deleveraging across the record margin debt base.
triggers: Margin call cascade from sharp initial decline
Private credit contagion spreading to banks
Liquidity crisis in semi-liquid fund structures

recommendation_framework.conservative_investors: Reduce equity allocation to below benchmark. Increase cash/short-duration bonds. Avoid leveraged positions. Focus on quality names with strong balance sheets.
recommendation_framework.moderate_investors: Maintain neutral allocation but add portfolio hedges (put spreads on S&P 500). Diversify internationally. Avoid concentration in high-multiple tech names.
recommendation_framework.aggressive_investors: Can maintain positions but with strict stop-losses. Consider reducing margin usage given record systemic leverage. The Fear & Greed 'Fear' reading suggests short-term dips may be buyable, but long-term valuation headwinds are significant.

overall_assessment.historical_context: Current conditions most closely resemble late 1999 (extreme valuations) combined with elements of late 2007 (leverage excess, credit stress). However, unlike those periods, near-term sentiment is already fearful (contrarian bullish) and technical indicators are neutral rather than euphoric. The key risk is that structural imbalances can persist for extended periods before correcting abruptly.
overall_assessment.disclaimer: This assessment is algorithmic and for informational purposes only. It is not investment advice. Market crashes are inherently unpredictable and this model has significant limitations. Always consult a qualified financial advisor before making investment decisions.
